title: audit report
description: Schema for an audit.report.json file — machine-readable output of /ib-audit. Designed so that other tools (a CI job, a review dashboard, a Slack bot) can consume audit results without parsing human-readable text. Every run of /ib-audit produces exactly one audit report.

run_id.description: Unique identifier for this audit run. Convention: '<iso8601>-<short_hash>' (e.g., '2026-04-09T14-22-00Z-a7c3').

generated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when this audit report was generated.

workbook.description: Filename of the workbook the audit ran against.

workbook_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the workbook at audit time. A mismatch against the provenance file's workbook_hash surfaces as a WARN.

workbook_last_recalc.description: Best-effort timestamp of the workbook's last full recalculation. Used to compute workbook_recalc_staleness_hours.

workbook_recalc_staleness_hours.description: Hours elapsed between workbook_last_recalc and generated_at. The audit emits a WARN when this exceeds 24 (configurable).

specs.description: Paths (relative or absolute) of the spec files included in this audit run.

exceptions_file.description: Optional path to the deck.exceptions.json file used for this audit run. Null or omitted if no exceptions file was provided.

summary.description: Counts of findings by severity. The audit command's exit code is 0 when fail == 0 and 1 otherwise.

findings.description: List of individual audit findings, in the order they were generated. May be empty if no checks fired.

severity.description: Finding severity. PASS findings are usually omitted from the findings array (they're counted in summary.pass); FAIL, WARN, and INFO are always listed.

check.description: Check identifier. The initial set of 10 checks is defined in docs/v0.2-scope.md section 6. New checks may be added in v0.3+ without breaking this schema.

slide_id.description: Identifier of the slide this finding applies to (when applicable — not every check is slide-scoped).

metric.description: Metric name (e.g., 'EBITDA'). Present for slide-scoped findings.

period.description: Period label (e.g., 'FY2025A'). Present for slide-scoped findings.

expected.description: The value as it appears in the spec. Type matches the spec's value type (number or string).

actual.description: The value as it appears in the workbook. Type matches the spec's value type (number or string).

source_ref.description: The source_ref that was evaluated to produce this finding. Useful for debugging mapping issues.

message.description: Human-readable message describing the finding. Always present.

exception_matched.description: When this finding was downgraded from FAIL to INFO by a matching entry in deck.exceptions.json, this field carries the exception id. Null otherwise.
